title: Joins, Cardinality, Unmatched Rows, and Fanout Control · Stakeholder Data Mission
summary: A support operations lead. The lead needs deterministic queue, ownership, backlog, and effort queries that preserve unmatched teams and prevent fanout. Planned artifact: Deliver Support Queue Query Pack with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Create deterministic multi-key ordering and evaluate offset and keyset pagination tradeoffs.
Reconcile totals, denominators, missing groups, and changed cases before publishing an aggregate claim.
Use left, right, or full outer-join reasoning to retain required unmatched entities.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Fanout Diagnosis
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-fanout-diagnosis-7a4rsn" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; join relationship, unmatched-row, or fanout evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Fanout Diagnosis.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Fanout Diagnosis into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Fanout Diagnosis, keep Fanout Diagnosis connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-fanout-diagnosis-7a4rsn
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
WITH effort AS (
  SELECT ticket_id, SUM(minutes_spent) AS minutes
  FROM ticket_events GROUP BY ticket_id
)
SELECT tickets.ticket_id, COALESCE(effort.minutes, 0) AS minutes
FROM tickets
LEFT JOIN effort ON effort.ticket_id = tickets.ticket_id
ORDER BY tickets.ticket_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
